chapter_title.en: Chapter 13 – The witnessing upon the reversion

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/123)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/123)
text: الَّذِي يُرَاجِعُ وَ لَمْ يُشْهِدْ قَالَ يُشْهِدُ أَحَبُّ إِلَيَّ وَ لَا أَرَى بِالَّذِي صَنَعَ بَأْساً .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Hammad, from Al Halby,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> regarding the one who reverts (back to his wife) and does not keep witnesses. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Witnessing is more beloved to me<sup>asws</sup>, and I<sup>asws</sup> do not see any problem with what he has done’.
en.sarwar: 1. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Hammad from al-Halabiy who has said the following: “About the case of a man who goes back to his wife without witness, abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al- Salam, has said, ‘I like if he arranged for witness; however, what he has done is not unlawful.’ ”
